response:
To find the **greatest common divisor (GCD)** of the numbers $106029$ and $38115$, we use the **Euclidean algorithm**.

---

### Step 1: Apply the Euclidean Algorithm

We repeatedly apply the division algorithm:

$$
\begin{align*}
106029 \div 38115 &= 2 \quad \text{remainder } 106029 - 2 \cdot 38115 = 106029 - 76230 = 29799 \\
38115 \div 29799 &= 1 \quad \text{remainder } 38115 - 29799 = 8316 \\
29799 \div 8316 &= 3 \quad \text{remainder } 29799 - 3 \cdot 8316 = 29799 - 24948 = 4851 \\
8316 \div 4851 &= 1 \quad \text{remainder } 8316 - 4851 = 3465 \\
4851 \div 3465 &= 1 \quad \text{remainder } 4851 - 3465 = 1386 \\
3465 \div 1386 &= 2 \quad \text{remainder } 3465 - 2 \cdot 1386 = 3465 - 2772 = 693 \\
1386 \div 693 &= 2 \quad \text{remainder } 1386 - 2 \cdot 693 = 1386 - 1386 = 0
\end{align*}
$$

---

### Step 2: Stop when the remainder is 0

The last non-zero remainder is **693**, so:

$$
\gcd(106029, 38115) = \boxed{693}
$$

---

### Final Answer:

$$
\boxed{693}
$$
